feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Closing Journal Entries Are Missing in Generated e-Ledger TXT File – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Editi
entry.content:
Hello Community,

We are experiencing an issue in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition during the e-Ledger generation process.

When we generate the e-Ledger file via the “Run Statutory Reports” application, the closing journal entries are not included in the generated TXT file.

We have already maintained the year-end closing document type as “ZK” in the related configuration areas. However, the closing entries are still missing in the output file.

Steps to reproduce:

  1. Open the “Run Statutory Reports” application.
  2. Select the related reporting period (December 2025).
  3. Execute the e-Ledger generation process.
  4. Download the generated TXT file.
  5. Check the file content.

Actual result:
The closing journal entries are missing in the TXT output.

Expected result:
The closing journal entries should be included in the generated e-Ledger TXT file.

This is a productive system issue and critical for us, since the e-Ledger must be submitted to GİB within the legal deadline.

Has anyone experienced a similar issue or found a solution for this scenario?

Thank you.

Melissa.

entry.title: Issue of adding serial numbers to sales return delivery orders via API interface
entry.content: Today I created a return sales order and a corresponding return delivery document in our system.This return sales order contains materials managed with serial numbers. I know serial numbers can be added to the delivery document via transaction code VL02N. I would like to confirm whether it is possible to add serial numbers to return delivery documents through system interfaces.

entry.title: Activation of duplicate Business Partner check using the same Tax ID (CPF and CNPJ)
entry.content: I would like to understand the configuration required to activate the validation feature that prevents the creation of a Business Partner with the same CPF or CNPJ in the Brazil localization for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

entry.title: How to check the field values for FPAYHX-REF used in payment medium
entry.content:
Hi SAP Team,

I am using standard CGI_CT DME structure for payment medium. in some of the nodes the soure filed is mapped as FPAYHX-REF  user defined field (eg FPAYHX-REF07, FPAYHX-REF05). but we cannot find the exact field in the system.

SO can you support to find the standard fields mapped for FPAYHX-REF.

Regards 

Kalee

entry.title: Different Supplier Details in Withholding Tax Certificate (Thailand Localization)
entry.content:
My case is that we receive supplier invoices from Company AAA and generate cheque payments to Company AAA as normal. However, the supplier requires the withholding tax certificate to be issued under Company BBB (including Company BBB’s details such as address and tax ID) instead of Company AAA.

Is it possible to specify a different supplier specifically for the withholding tax certificate in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud? Or is there any standard solution/function that can support this scenario?

entry.title: What is the data source for cost element group
entry.content:
In the Custom CDS Views app, which data source will give the details of the Cost Element Group?

What CDS View will return Cost Element Group information?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Decimal Rounding in FI invoices
entry.content:
Dear Expert , 

My client need the integer amount when calculate the tax amount in Finance invoice (incoming and Outgoing Invocie)

eg.

Net Amount = 213150

Tax amount= (213150*5%)=10657.5 (But my client want the integer amount - 10658)

Which config need to do to get this? Please advice. 

 

Thanks and Best Regards,

Hnin Oo

entry.title: I want to change the default order of the code and text for extended fields.
entry.content:
I want to change the default order of the code and text for extended fields.

The image shows journal entry data exported to Excel.

Standard fields (Supplier column) and extended fields (*Customer) are listed side by side.

The display order is reversed between the standard and extended fields: Code (Name) <=> Name (Code).

I want to unify this. Please tell me how to do this (I want the extended fields to follow the standard field order).

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Interest calcuation on Customer Open Items with 30/60/90 grace days
entry.content:
Hi,

Business requirement is to calcualte interest from Due date +30/60/90 Grace Days, but system behaviour is that when Tolerance days is configured, if the key date of calculation of interest falls within that Tolerance days, system is not calcuating interest, once the tolerance days got completed, interest is getting calculated from Net Due Date. Please assist.

entry.title: Worker Concept in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I'm researching Authorization in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and have several questions about Workers.

 1. What is the definition of a Worker in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud? Why is a Worker required in the system?

 2. What is the process for creating a Worker in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?

 3. How can a Worker be removed from the system? If the Worker cannot be removed, what are the reasons?

Thank you for your answer.

QuocDT5

entry.title: Issue in Milestone Activity Confirmation at Order Level
entry.content:
Hello SAP Team,

We observed an issue while performing milestone activity confirmation at the production order level.

Issue Description:
When we execute order confirmation with milestone confirmation functionality enabled, the system is confirming only the last operation activity parameters automatically. However, the activity parameters for all previous operations are not getting confirmed.

Expected Behavior:
As per milestone confirmation logic, once the milestone operation is confirmed, the system should automatically confirm all preceding operations along with their respective activity parameters.

Actual Behavior:
Only the last operation activity parameters are getting confirmed, while activity parameters for earlier operations remain unconfirmed.

Test Scenario:

  1. Create a production order with multiple operations.
  2. Maintain milestone confirmation for the last operation.
  3. Release the order.
  4. Perform confirmation at order level for the milestone operation.
  5. Check operation/activity confirmation status.

Observation:<

entry.title: Issue with I_OrganizationAddress Not Available for External API Creation
entry.content:
While creating an External API, the view I_OrganizationAddress is not available for selection. However, the same view can be successfully selected when creating a CDS View.

Does anyone know why this view is not exposed or eligible for External API creation? Are there any prerequisites or configurations required to make it available?

entry.title: Howto maintain fiscal year variant SZ so that period information does not need tobe maintained year
entry.content:
Background:
To reserve flexibility for the future development of our group (e.g., non-calendar fiscal years), we have chosen fiscal year variant SZ. We intend to use SZ as a variant that follows the calendar year (natural months). In the future, for countries with non-calendar fiscal years, we will copy SZ and adjust the configuration accordingly.

Issue:
During a previous implementation for another customer, we also selected SZ. At that time, the field "C" was checked, but the field "Year-dependent" was not checked. As a result, for fiscal year variant SZ, every time we needed to post to a new fiscal year, we had to manually maintain the period information in the posting periods field.

Question:
When maintaining fiscal year variant SZ, if both the field "C" and the field "Year-dependent" are checked, will it eliminate the need to maintain the period information per fiscal year?

entry.title: Issue with S4 app - Schedule Order Acknowledgement output for Purchase Orders
entry.content:
Hello Team, We are currently testing the S/4HANA app “Schedule Order Acknowledgement Output” for Purchase Orders, specifically for the Order Acknowledgement Reminder scenario.

As per the Confirmation Control configuration, the setup is as follows:

  • Monitoring period: 2 days
  • Reference date: PO date + monitoring time
  • “Subject to Reminder” checkbox: Enabled

Based on this configuration, we need to understand the application behavior for the following points:

  1. Reminder Triggering Logic
    We expect to receive reminders until the AB Order Acknowledgement confirmation is received from the supplier. However, we observe inconsistent behavior:

    • For some POs, reminders are not received at all
    • For other POs, reminders are received daily

  2. PO Selection Criteria
    We would like to understand how the app selects Purchase Orders for sending reminders.

  3. Reminder Stop Condition
    Our understanding is that reminders should stop only after the AB Order Acknowledgement confirmation is received from the supplier.
    Please clarify:

    • Is this the only stop condition?
    • Is there a way to restrict or stop reminders when no AB Order Acknowledgement is received from the supplier over a certain period?

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Combined Credit/Debit Note Posting Against Multiple Invoices for Customer Rate Difference
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We are currently using the Customer Rate Difference Bills Auto Debit process in SAP GROW / SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

The existing process is working as expected. However, we have a new business requirement for selected customers.

Current Process:
Credit notes and debit notes are generated based on POAM / rate difference adjustments, and the current SAP GROW process is functioning correctly.

New Requirement:
For a few customers, instead of posting separate credit and debit notes invoice-wise, we need the system to calculate the combined total amount across all relevant credit and debit adjustments and then post only one consolidated credit note or debit note against multiple invoices.

For example:

  • Multiple invoices may have credit note and/or debit note adjustments.
  • The system should total all applicable credit and debit values for the customer.
  • Based on the net amount:
    • If the final amount is credit, create one consolidated credit note.
    • If the final amount is debit, create one consolidated debit note.
  • The document should still have reference/linkage to the multiple original invoices.

Business Need:
This is required for specific customers who do not want multiple adjustment documents. They require a single consolidated debit/credit note for all applicable invoices.

Questions for SAP:

  1. Is this requirement supported in standard SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition / GROW with SAP?
  2. Can one credit memo or debit memo be created with reference to multiple invoices in this scenario?
  3. Is it possible to net both credit and debit adjustments and create only one final credit/debit memo document?
  4. If not available in standard, is there a recommended SAP-approved approach using:
    • Configuration,
    • Billing document split control,
    • Custom logic,
    • Key user extensibility,
    • Side-by-side extension,
    • Or another standard scope item?
  5. Are there any limitations related to billing type, document flow, tax calculation, accounting posting, invoice reference, or audit compliance for this requirement?

Expected Outcome:
whether this requirement is achievable in SAP GROW standard functionality or whether an enhancement/custom extension is required. If possible, please provide the recommended solution approach and relevant configuration/extensibility options.

entry.title: Issue Maintaining 19-Digit Bank Account Number for Customer Master Data in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We are facing an issue in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud while maintaining customer bank details through the “Manage Business Partner Master Data” / "Maintain Business Partner" app.

The customer has a 19-digit bank account number, however, the system currently allows entry of only up to 18 digits for the bank account number field.

To resolve this, we tried updating the bank account number length from 18 to 19 in the country-specific settings (attached image for reference), but the change is not taking effect and the system is still restricting the entry to 18 digits.

Could you please suggest:

  1. Is it possible in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to maintain a bank account number with more than 18 digits in the Business Partner app?
  2. Is there any configuration, SSCUI, or workaround available to achieve this requirement?
  3. Are there any SAP notes or standard limitations related to this behavior?

Any guidance would be appreciated.

 

Thanks and Regards,

Rohini Dutta

entry.title: Requirement for CDS View – India GST Tax Breakup
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We require a CDS view for India GST tax breakup, covering CGST, SGST, IGST, and UGST components. This is needed for the development of the Purchase Register and Sales Register for our client.

We request your support on this at the earliest.

Regards,

Mayur Patil.

entry.title: Assign Customer/Supplier to Sales Organization
entry.content:
I have an OEM that sends 862 JIT EDI for Summarized JIT calls AND 850 EDI for creating Sales Orders. The SAP Sold to and Supplier # is the same. Ex. 1000010 and 01133001

In Assign Customer/Supplier to Sales Organization how do I assign 1000010 and 01133001 to Sales Order type OR AND to SA type LZJ?  

In Test Scripts 53X and 2EM for LZJ JIT scheduling agreements, there is a step to Assign Customer/Supplier to Sales Organization - S_ER9_52000999?

And in Test Script 2EL for Integration with External Buyers, the Assign Customer/Supplier to Sales Org is also in the steps. 

Assign Customer to Sales Org looks like the VOE2 transaction that was required for Inbound ORDERS Idoc processing prior to Public Cloud.

Is it still required to be set up for Order Type OR Sales Order integration when When using the Sales Order (A2A) -  SalesOrderBulkRequest_In SOAP for example?  

I also have multiple Supplier Codes with the OEM. ex. 01133001 & 01133011. What Node and Element should contain the Supplier Code (01133001) in the API for Sales Orders?

entry.title: Which CDS view provides Commodity Code data for a product in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the Commodity Code field is visible in the Manage Product Master Data app, but it is not clear which released CDS view exposes this data for consumption in custom reports or SD form templates (Sales Order, Outbound Delivery, Tax Invoice).

Which CDS view should be used to fetch Commodity Code (Customs Tariff Number) data for a product?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Cannot create Party Entitled to Dispose
entry.content:
I am creating a new company code and have gotten most of the steps complete. I am stuck on creating the Party Entitled to Dispose. I have created the Business Partner exactly the same as the business partner for the original company code. When I go to Set Warehouse Address and add the new warehouse in, I get the error message "The system can't determine the party entitled to dispose." 

Old Company code = 1010. New Company Code = 2010

Things I have checked: 

  • Warehouse is EWM Managed
  • Storage Locations are attached to warehouse
  • Company code 2010 exists and is active
  • Country, currency, and address are maintained
  • Fiscal year variant assigned
  • Posting period variant assigned
  • 2010 → same controlling area as 1010
  • Every plant used in logistics is assigned to 2010
  • No plant is left assigned to 1010 accidentally
  • No duplicate plant assignment across incompatible company codes
  • Storage locations exist under correct plant
  • PED exists for warehouse
  • PED is assigned to:
    • Warehouse number
    • Plant
    • Storage locations (if required)
  • PED is ACTIVE (not draft / inactive)
  • PED is linked to correct organizational unit (often shipping point or warehouse org unit)
  • BP has both Supplier and Customer role
  • Company code segment exists for 2010
  • Purchasing / shipping data is maintained
  • BP is not missing “plant-level” logistics roles

I know I am missing something obvious/simple but I just can't figure it out. Any help is greatly appreciated. 

Vicki
